purpose: Attempt to isolate a HUMAN server-side signal from server_page_view using GA4's Bot and AI Tool dimensions, to test whether the Mar-Apr 2026 client decline was measurement (humans stable) or real (humans down). Conclusion: GA's Bot/AI-Tool flags do NOT reliably separate humans from AI agents - even the cleanest proxy (Bot=false AND AI-Tool=false) grows implausibly (2-4x in 2 months), so it is still dominated by undetected AI/agent traffic. Server-side GA cannot settle the question.

AI Tool.note: ALSO boolean-ish: (not set)/false/true, NOT tool names

monthly._comment: server_page_view Event count by month. Compare client_docs (page_view) against the server cuts.

verdict: Inconclusive from server side. Client docs page_view halved Feb->Apr (448k->216k) while EVERY server cut (incl. Bot=false AND AI-Tool=false) roughly DOUBLED. Real human traffic does not 2x in two months, so the server 'human proxy' is contaminated by undetected agents - GA4 cannot isolate humans here. The May Cookiebot cliff is unambiguously measurement; the Mar-Apr decline coincides with the Feb infra/tracking cluster (Netlify cutover, GTM-only) and is most likely substantially measurement, but the exact real-vs-measurement split needs a consent-independent, properly bot-filtered source (Netlify Analytics / server logs), not GA4.
